metadata.title: VIRAL CAPSID GEOMETRY (VCG)
metadata.description:
The Caspar–Klug triangulation number T = h² + hk + k² is identified as the norm of an Eisenstein integer, establishing viral capsid geometry as an instance of the closure theorem from the Iterative Geometric Theory (IGT) framework. Three theorems and three propositions are derived with zero free parameters: (1) the Loeschian Exclusion Theorem shows that only integers representable as Eisenstein norms can be T-numbers, confirmed against ~930 structures in VIPERdb with all 29 non-Loeschian integers from 2–50 absent; (2) the Chirality Classification identifies handedness with non-self-conjugate Eisenstein integers, confirmed by the laevo/dextro assignment of T = 7 capsids; (3) the Pentameric Closure Count recovers the 12-pentamer rule from Euler's formula; (4) the Stability Hierarchy derives a three-class system (achiral, primitive chiral, composite chiral) from Jensen's inequality on mismatch strain energy, confirmed across the VIPERdb census; (5) the M₇ Eigenstructure Prediction derives the specific dihedral partition {A}, {B,G}, {C,F}, {D,E} for the seven quasi-equivalent positions in T = 7 capsids, matching bacteriophage P22 scaffolding discrimination exactly (1 of 35 possible partitions); (6) the Chiral Seam Prediction gives a quantitative 3D mismatch angle Δ ≈ 13.4° for T = 7d capsids, testable by high-resolution cryo-EM.

The Eisenstein prime criterion partitions the seven-prime basis {2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17} of the RICC framework into capsid primes {3, 7, 13} and non-capsid primes {2, 5, 11, 17}, with the capsid primes corresponding exactly to the heptagonal branch of the gauge dressing chain established in the Standard Model Derivation and Standard Model Completion. Viral capsid geometry independently selects the same number-theoretic partition that the Standard Model gauge structure does. Five of six predictions are confirmed with zero refutations.
